name: Cheese Enchiladas with Mole Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 15oz can diced tomatoes (pureed would work too for a smoother sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the mole sauce, heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté onions until soft and golden, about 12 minutes.
2. Add garlic, chili powder, sugar, and cinnamon; cook for about a minute.
3. Stir in diced tomatoes (or pureed), water, cocoa powder, and peanut butter. Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In two 9x13 inch baking dishes, spread about a cup of mole sauce in the bottom.
3. Place tortillas in the center of each dish.
4. Add 2 1/2 Tablespoons of cheese along the center of each tortilla.
5. Roll up the tortillas and place seam side down in the pan.
6. Reserve about 2/3 cup of cheese for topping.
7. Repeat with remaining ingredients, arranging 8 enchiladas per dish.
8. Pour remaining mole sauce over the enchiladas, then sprinkle 1/3 cup of cheese on top.
9. Bake frozen enchiladas for 40 minutes or unfrozen enchiladas for 20 minutes, until the sauce is bubbly and the cheese melts.
Serves: 8
